plan_metadata.name: Feature Verify Scaffold Works For Dynamically Added Template

steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface, initiating the creation of a new agent by declaring instructions and actions.
Click the "Weather Agent" option in the dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface, selecting a weather forecast agent built with Microsoft 365 Agents SDK and LangChain.
Click the "OpenAI" option in the dropdown menu under "Select a service to access LLMs" within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface in Visual Studio Code.
Type text 'fake' into the OpenAI Key input field at the top of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Press 'Enter' to confirm the OpenAI Key input in the password entry dialog within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension for Visual Studio Code.
Click the "TypeScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown menu at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.

Click the "Default folder" option in the folder selection dropdown at the top of the "Workspace Folder" dialog in Visual Studio Code, setting the project root location to "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects".
Type the text ${{var:app_name:vscuse_app_#####}} into the "Application Name" input field in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension for Visual Studio Code, as shown in the top command bar.
Press the Enter key to confirm the input in the "Application Name" text field at the top of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Use the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+~ in Visual Studio Code to open a new integrated terminal at the bottom of the workspace window, while viewing the "Preview README.md" file in the project folder.
Type "pwd" into the integrated terminal's bash prompt in Visual Studio Code.
Press the Enter key in the VS Code integrated terminal where the "pwd" command is typed, within the project workspace.
